q: Echoes are produced where a sound wave is reflected from a large and hard surface.
An echo is a repetition of sound due to the reflection of sound.
Reflection of sound violates the laws of reflection.
Rugs and carpets increase the amount of sound reflecting off floor surfaces.
Echolocation is the special ability of emitting sounds and interpreting the echoes.
Bats are known to use echolocation to navigate and locate their prey.
A reverberation is a reflection of light or sound waves.
The sound bouncing around in a large speaker is an example of a reverberation.
Padding and carpets absorb the incident sound and reduce the amount of sound reflecting.
Walls in buildings are covered with padding to increase the effect of echo.
